suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention for agent/LLM backend wiring in production code. Currently with_llm() constructors exist but are not wired to any production code path. Document the expected pattern for how agents should receive their LLM backends.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature fix-doc-writer-llm: DocWriterAgent::with_llm() exists but is never called in production code. PlannerAgent/GeneratorAgent similarly have with_llm() constructors not wired to production.

rejectedObservations.observation: Naming discrepancy: feature description refers to 'tool_call_results' but actual field is 'tool_calls'
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a documentation/naming issue in the feature description, not a code defect. The implementation correctly uses the actual field name.

rejectedObservations.observation: Hardcoded thresholds (0.65 warning, 0.75 condensation) not loaded from settings.json
rejectedObservations.reason: Pre-existing code issue from commit 3a8c017, not introduced by this fix feature. The fix did not change this code.
This is not a blocking issue - workers proceed without the skill. The observation is informational.

reviews.summary: Fix correctly populates tool_call_results and total_tokens. Implementation extracts tool_name and arguments from ReactStep.action via parse_action helper, captures token usage from LLM.chat(). All 188 tests pass.

DocWriterAgent.execute() now implements LLM-based documentation generation via llm.chat(). with_llm() constructor and load_system_prompt() properly implemented. Build and tests pass. Non-blocking issues: unused prompt variable, silent empty response handling.

ContextCondensation implementation verified correct. All three required methods (needs_condensation(), condense(), utilization()) work correctly. This fix addressed a process issue (prior worker claimed credit for pre-existing code), not an implementation issue.
